focus: correctness — published generation consistency, crash safety, hash freshness, eligibility-first RRF, vector degradation, adapter modes, and fail-closed paths

findingDetails.title: A worker crash permanently strands a vector job in running
findingDetails.evidence: The drain selects only pending/failed jobs and persists running before awaiting the embedder; no lease or recovery path makes a crash-orphaned row selectable again, and unchanged indexing treats that row as existing work.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Exact search across _db/_engine found the only running claim and no stale-running recovery; only explicit rebuildVectorProjection resets jobs.
findingDetails.claim: A process exit after claim leaves the vector job permanently unselectable in normal operation.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Searched all target modules/tests for leases, stale claim reset, startup reconciliation, and worker-crash coverage; none exists.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: Explicit projection rebuild repairs it, but normal crash recovery does not invoke that maintenance path.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: A bounded stale-running reconciliation path and process-interruption regression test are added.

findingDetails.title: Persistent queries ignore an explicitly requested generation
findingDetails.evidence: Query validation always uses the current pointer and checks identity only for cursors; generationHash is absent from fingerprint and comparison while hydration enforces it.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Exact search found requested-generation enforcement only in hydration and cursor handling; no query test supplies generationHash.
findingDetails.claim: The persistent query silently substitutes current generation for caller-supplied generationHash.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Searched query, adapter, hydration, and tests for mismatch/retained-generation handling; only cursors and hydration enforce identity.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: The v1 query may intend current-only behavior, but phase-001 native design includes a generation pin and phase 003 documents no exception.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: The contract explicitly removes query pins or implementation validates/reopens requested retained generations with tests.

findingDetails.title: The aggregate hash includes the mutable slug locator
findingDetails.evidence: Artifact relativePath includes slug and is hashed despite the research contract requiring style-relative paths, so locator-only renames rotate generation identity.
findingDetails.scopeProof: The single aggregate-hash producer and artifact path producer were traced; retrieval hash remains separate.

results.summary: Three correctness mismatches survived direct implementation comparison.
Focused correctness claims checked; complete four-phase matrix deferred to traceability.

noveltyJustification: All three findings are new in the empty lineage registry; each came from direct producer/consumer tracing beyond the passing in-process regression cases.
edgeCases: code graph unavailable; direct search used
selected 17 focused tests do not simulate worker process death
full checklist matrix deferred
unrelated pre-existing dirty paths untouched
ruledOut: partial relational generation after mid-commit exception

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: The target is a persistent SQLite/FTS/vector subsystem with crash, publication, migration, and generation-consistency invariants.

targetSelection.selectionReason: These are the correctness-critical surfaces named by the rendered iteration prompt and phase-003 P0 requirements.
omittedHighRiskTargets: full command registration and phase-004 interface surfaces deferred by one-dimension scope
full-corpus SLO deferred by correctness focus

searchLedger.hypothesis: A process death after durable job claim is automatically recovered.

searchActions.queryOrPath: vectors.mjs drain claim/select and indexer missing-job query
searchActions.result: Claims become running before await; selectors exclude running and no lease reset exists.
Only claim/completion/failure transitions exist; no stale-running recovery or crash test.

searchLedger.rationale: Normal operation cannot make a crash-orphaned running row selectable.

searchLedger.hypothesis: A requested query generation is either pinned or rejected.

searchActions.queryOrPath: query fingerprint/generation validation and hydration comparison
searchActions.result: Query ignores request.generationHash; hydration rejects mismatch.
No query-generation enforcement or test exists outside cursor checks.

searchLedger.rationale: The query can silently substitute current state for caller-pinned state.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Aggregate content identity excludes mutable slug locators.

searchActions.result: The slug-prefixed path is directly length-framed into the aggregate hash.

searchLedger.rationale: Slug-only moves churn generation identity contrary to research hash framing.

searchLedger.hypothesis: A mid-commit failure can expose partial relational state.

searchActions.queryOrPath: BEGIN IMMEDIATE through PUBLISH rollback boundary
searchActions.result: Mutation and pointer-row publication share one transaction with rollback on error.
17/17 passed, including mid-commit failure injection and recovery.

searchLedger.rationale: The tested transaction boundary preserves prior generation and hashes.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Direct code and failure-injection evidence agree.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Ineligible candidates can enter lanes, raw scores affect fusion, or lexical fallback blocks on vectors.

searchActions.queryOrPath: eligibility and lane inputs, weightedRrf, pagination, channel fallback
searchActions.result: Every lane receives eligible IDs, fusion uses rank, and empty/failed vector lane degrades locally.
Eligibility, RRF, cursor, invalid generation, and failed-vector cases passed.

searchLedger.rationale: Implementation and focused regression evidence agree.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: No contradictory path was found within the declared retrieval surface.

focus: security — SQLite/file trust boundaries, generation pointer validation, corpus and hydration containment, hostile query/vector inputs, exemplar trust, mutation boundaries, and alias recursion

findingDetails.title: The generation pointer accepts a symlinked database outside its publication directory
findingDetails.evidence: Pointer validation constrains only a textual basename, follows an accepted symlink through existsSync/DatabaseSync, and never binds pointer.generationHash to the opened database; a focused probe confirmed pointer_symlink_accepted=true.
findingDetails.scopeProof: The single pointer resolver feeds persistent query and hydration; writer-side staging/rollback validation exists, but exact search and schema tests found no reader-side realpath containment, hash binding, or pointer-symlink test.
findingDetails.claim: A basename-only pointer can resolve through a symlink outside the generation directory and its declared generationHash is not bound to the opened database.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Reviewed staged publication and rollback validation at indexer.mjs:1010-1173, searched pointer/symlink tests, and ran focused schema/retrieval suites.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: The directory may be writable only by a principal that can already replace generation files.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Prove/enforce an immutable trusted directory for every caller, or add read-side realpath containment and pointer/database generation binding.

findingDetails.title: Persistent retrieval accepts arbitrarily large query vectors
findingDetails.evidence: The request fingerprint serializes the full vector and vectorLane copies it while checking only finite/non-empty values; a 20,000-element focused probe returned ok=true although embedder output is capped at 16,384.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Exact search found the only query-vector validation at retrieval.mjs:210-215; the dimension cap applies only to output vectors, and tests contain no oversized query case.
findingDetails.claim: The synchronous persistent query path accepts and duplicates an unbounded caller-controlled queryVector.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Searched all queryVector/MAX_VECTOR_DIMENSIONS references and ran retrieval tests; no upstream or query-side cap was found.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: Current callers may construct small vectors internally rather than expose the API to untrusted network input.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Demonstrate an enforced upstream vector-size cap on every query caller; otherwise validate dimensions and serialized size before fingerprinting.

results.summary: Two security trust/input boundaries fail closed-contract comparison.

evidence: focused probes: pointer symlink and oversized vector accepted
results.summary: Hydration, rights, mutation, and alias claims checked; pointer/vector gaps remain and the full matrix is deferred to traceability.

noveltyJustification: Two new P1 security findings were demonstrated beyond 26 passing focused tests: reader-side pointer symlink acceptance and oversized query-vector acceptance. Prior correctness findings were carried without duplication.
edgeCases: pointer exploitability depends on unrepresented deployment directory permissions
query API is local/in-process with no network exposure found
prompt-injection behavior has static contract evidence but no runtime/model harness
structural-impact analysis unavailable; direct evidence used
first probe reproduced findings but cleanup double-close exited non-zero; corrected probe completed cleanly

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: The target combines persistent SQLite publication, filesystem hydration, vector input, and command trust/mutation boundaries.

targetSelection.selectionReason: These are the security-critical surfaces named by the rendered iteration prompt and phase-003/004 P0 requirements.

searchLedger.hypothesis: A published pointer cannot redirect readers outside its directory and is bound to its declared generation.

searchActions.queryOrPath: pointer writer/resolver to persistent query and hydration opens
searchActions.result: Writer validates ordinary files; resolver checks basename/exists only and ignores generationHash after shape validation.

searchActions.queryOrPath: basename symlink in temporary database directory
searchActions.result: pointer_symlink_accepted=true
No pointer containment/hash-binding test found.

searchLedger.rationale: Reader-side path and generation binding are absent and the escaping target is accepted.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Query vectors are rejected before serialization when they exceed the supported dimension bound.

searchActions.result: The full vector is serialized and copied; only empty/non-finite values are rejected.
The 16,384 cap applies only to embedder outputs; query tests use two dimensions.
oversized_vector_accepted=true length=20000

searchLedger.rationale: The public synchronous query path has no vector dimension/size bound.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Static traversal, escaping symlinks, stale hashes, or unknown exact-reuse rights can expose corpus content.

searchActions.queryOrPath: index/manifest/hydration realpath, hash, byte, and rights guards
searchActions.result: Containment is checked against corpus/style realpaths; stale hashes and unknown rights refuse.
26/26 passed, including traversal, escaping symlink, stale hash, rights, generation, and byte-cap cases.

searchLedger.rationale: Direct guards and adversarial tests agree.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: No contradictory static path was found in the declared corpus/hydration surfaces.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Untrusted exemplars can override workflow authority, advisory commands mutate, or aliases recursively dispatch commands.

searchActions.queryOrPath: shared authority/grounding/mutation contract and canonical/alias wrappers
searchActions.result: Reference material is explicitly untrusted; advisory tools are read-only; aliases execute in current context and prohibit public-command invocation.
9/9 command tests passed, including nested dispatch and silent amendment rejection; prompt-injection resistance remains static contract evidence only.

searchLedger.rationale: Static command and tool boundaries are coherent; runtime/model prompt-injection behavior is unavailable in scope.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: No static authority, mutation, or alias-recursion defect was found.

findingDetails.title: Phase 001 marks a ten-iteration research task complete after only seven iterations
findingDetails.detail: Phase 001 marks a ten-iteration research task complete even though the authoritative handoff records only seven iterations and current strict validation fails.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Spec, tasks, checklist, synthesis, handoff, and parent map were reconciled; only checked completion evidence asserts ten executed iterations.

adjudication.claim: Checked ten-iteration execution contradicts the seven-iteration authoritative handoff.
adjudication.counterevidenceSought: Promoted synthesis and lineage artifact roster for omitted iterations.
adjudication.alternativeExplanation: Ten may have been a maximum, but T002 claims execution and the handoff records a stall.
adjudication.downgradeTrigger: Correct checked rows and cite a legal seven-iteration convergence stop.

findingDetails.title: Phase 002 checks both research lineages complete although GLM produced zero iterations
findingDetails.detail: Phase 002 marks both SOL and GLM lineages complete and converged although GLM produced zero iterations and the synthesis is SOL-only.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Spec, tasks, checklist, SOL synthesis, handoff, and parent map agree on SOL=20/GLM=0 except checked completion rows.

adjudication.claim: Both lineages are checked complete despite GLM failing before iteration one.
adjudication.counterevidenceSought: GLM delta, dissent, or successful secondary iteration in synthesis and handoff.
adjudication.alternativeExplanation: SOL may be sufficient, but the frozen requirement explicitly requires both lineages.
adjudication.downgradeTrigger: Amend requirements and checked rows or provide real GLM merge evidence.

findingDetails.title: Phase 003 claims a corpus-scale SLO was measured from a 20-style fixture
findingDetails.detail: Phase 003 marks the corpus-scale persistent-query SLO measured and proven although its test fixture contains only twenty styles and the handoff defers the full-corpus check.
findingDetails.scopeProof: REQ-007, tasks, checklist, the sole timing test, and handoff limitations were traced; no 1,290-style persistent benchmark exists.

adjudication.claim: A 20-style fixture is presented as proof against the same-corpus 1,290-style baseline.
adjudication.counterevidenceSought: Exact SLO/timing search and all 24 current DB tests.
adjudication.alternativeExplanation: The bounded test is useful regression evidence but not same-corpus proof.
adjudication.downgradeTrigger: Run and record the same full-corpus comparison or correct/defer the checked SLO claim.

findingDetails.title: The parent declares the four-phase program complete and verified while required evidence is false
findingDetails.detail: The parent declares all four phases complete and verified despite false checked research/SLO evidence, active required defects, and a failing current recursive strict validation.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Parent handoff criteria were reconciled against all four child matrices, current tests/checkers, strict validation, and the corrected active baseline.

adjudication.claim: Parent Complete is unsupported while mandatory child evidence is contradicted and active P1s remain.
adjudication.counterevidenceSought: Current DB, legacy, command tests/checkers, and recursive strict validation.
adjudication.alternativeExplanation: Complete may mean delivered, but the parent explicitly claims built and verified.
adjudication.downgradeTrigger: Reconcile child evidence/defects and strict validation or narrow the parent lifecycle claim.

findingDetails.title: Persistent DB maintenance has no owned operator surface or bounded generation-retention path
findingDetails.detail: Full builds retain uniquely named immutable generations, while publication, rollback, vector repair, and queue drain are library-only with no production operator owner or prune policy.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Exact search found the maintenance exports consumed only internally or by tests; CLI has legacy build/query/hydrate only and generation deletion targets temporary/building paths.

adjudication.claim: Persistent DB publication and repair are library-only surfaces, and repeated full builds retain uniquely named generation files without a bounded retention or prune path.
adjudication.counterevidenceSought: Searched all styles modules/tests and packet docs for production consumers, operator commands, generation pruning, retention policy, or a runbook; none was found.
adjudication.alternativeExplanation: Deployments can script the callable exports and legacy remains default, but this supplies neither a stable tested maintenance owner nor bounded file retention.
adjudication.downgradeTrigger: Provide a documented/tested operator surface and explicit safe retention/pruning invariant.

traceabilityChecks.summary: Prior evidence-backed protocol failure carried; exhausted protocol was not re-run in maintainability.

Fixture-scale SLO gap is already represented by SOL-I003-P1-003 and was not duplicated.
Structural graph unavailable; graphless direct evidence used.
Unrelated dirty workspace paths were not modified.

findingDetails.title: The parent declares the four-phase program complete and verified while required evidence is false
findingDetails.detail: The parent still declares all phases built and verified despite contradicted child evidence and active P1 findings.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Current parent metadata and handoff gates remain inconsistent with stabilized child evidence.

adjudication.claim: The parent Complete status is unsupported while mandatory child evidence is contradicted and active P1s remain.
adjudication.counterevidenceSought: Reconciled current parent language with child evidence and reran 20 focused tests; delivery is green in areas but all claimed verification evidence is not.
adjudication.alternativeExplanation: Complete may mean delivered, but the parent explicitly says built and verified.
adjudication.downgradeTrigger: Reconciled evidence/defects or lifecycle language distinguishing delivery from verification.

findingDetails.title: Persistent DB maintenance has no owned operator surface or bounded generation-retention path
findingDetails.detail: Persistent publication, rollback, vector repair, and drain remain library/test-only while immutable generations have no prune policy.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Current export/consumer and prune searches find no production owner, runbook, or bounded generation cleanup.

adjudication.claim: Persistent DB publication/repair are library-only and repeated builds retain generations without bounded pruning.
adjudication.counterevidenceSought: Repeated current consumer and prune/retention searches across code, CLI, README, and tests; none exists.
adjudication.alternativeExplanation: Deployments can script exports and legacy remains default, but this is not an owned tested operator contract.
adjudication.downgradeTrigger: Documented/tested operator lifecycle plus a safe retention/pruning invariant.

findingDetails.title: The aggregate hash includes the mutable slug locator
findingDetails.detail: Artifact relativePath includes slug and is hashed, so locator-only renames rotate generation identity.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Current aggregate-hash and artifact-path producers remain unchanged; retrieval hash is separate.

traceabilityChecks.summary: Stabilization direct reads confirm all implementation/contract gaps and frozen evidence contradictions remain.
Stabilization direct reads confirm false checked evidence remains in phases 001-003 and parent completion remains unsupported.

noveltyJustification: Mandatory stabilization reproduced the existing P0=0, P1=9, P2=1 active baseline. No new or refined severity weight was claimed; all ten root causes, hashes, and fix boundaries were preserved.
edgeCases: Graph/structural impact unavailable; cited graphless fallback used.
Green 20/20 focused tests omit every active bug class's missing negative case.
Legacy default contains immediate persistent-path exposure.
Rollback needs one prior generation; absence of a bounded keep/prune policy is the defect.
All ten active findings retain distinct root causes and fix boundaries.
Unrelated dirty workspace paths were not modified.
ruledOut: collapse of crash recovery into operator maintenance
green focused tests resolve missing negative cases

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Mandatory stabilization spans persistent storage, vector jobs, query/hydration consumers, operator CLI/docs, frozen phase evidence, and parent release status.

selectedTargets: negative DB tests for each implementation bug class
operator maintenance and generation retention surfaces
targetSelection.selectionReason: The rendered stabilization prompt requires replaying every active finding, deduplicating root causes, preserving frozen specifications, and rechecking operator maintenance.
